briefing_summary: Today's briefing: 15 blog posts, 7 model updates, 1 Claude Code releases, 19 desktop updates, 7 Office plugin updates, 2 Chrome extension updates, 4 tweets, 1 SDK releases, 1 community discussions. Latest: v2.1.90.

ai_models.title: We launched our most capable Sonnet model yet, with a full upgrade of the model’s skills across coding, computer use, lo...
ai_models.summary: We launched our most capable Sonnet model yet, with a full upgrade of the model’s skills across coding, computer use, long-context reasoning, agent planning, knowledge work, and design. Sonnet 4.6 also features a 1M token context window in beta. Read our blog post for more information:Introducing Cl

ai_models.title: We’ve upgraded our smartest model and improved its coding skills. Read our blog post for more information:Introducing Cl...
ai_models.summary: We’ve upgraded our smartest model and improved its coding skills. Read our blog post for more information:Introducing Claude Opus 4.6.

office_plugins.title: Shared context across Excel and PowerPoint add-ins
office_plugins.summary: The Claude add-ins for Excel and PowerPoint now share a single conversation thread, eliminating repeated setup when switching between apps. Reusable Skills let teams save repeatable workflows as one-click actions. Available on Mac and Windows for all paid plans.

office_plugins.title: We’ve improved our Claude for Excel and Claude for PowerPoint add-ins. They can now share the full context of your conve...
office_plugins.summary: We’ve improved our Claude for Excel and Claude for PowerPoint add-ins. They can now share the full context of your conversation, so every action Claude takes in one application is informed by everything that’s happened in the other. We also added support for skills in the add-ins, and the ability fo

office_plugins.title: Use Claude for Excel and PowerPoint with an LLM gateway
office_plugins.summary: Use Claude for Excel and PowerPoint with an LLM gateway

office_plugins.title: Claude for PowerPoint — research preview
office_plugins.summary: Claude for PowerPoint launches as a research preview. It reads slide masters, layouts, fonts, and color schemes to generate or revise content while maintaining brand consistency. Creates native editable charts and diagrams. Available for Max, Team, and Enterprise plans.

office_plugins.title: Claude for Excel — now available for Pro subscribers
office_plugins.summary: Claude for Excel expands from Max/Enterprise to all Pro subscribers ($20/mo). Supports full spreadsheet functionality including pivot tables, chart modifications, and conditional formatting while keeping formulas and dependencies intact.

office_plugins.title: Claude for Excel — beta launch
office_plugins.summary: Anthropic releases Claude for Excel as a Microsoft Office add-in. Initially available in beta for Max and Enterprise plans. Claude can read, understand, and modify spreadsheets directly from a sidebar panel.

desktop.title: Computer use research preview in Cowork and Claude Code + Dispatch improvements
desktop.summary: Computer use research preview in Cowork and Claude Code + Dispatch improvements

desktop.title: Users on Pro and Max plans can give Claude access to computer use. Claude can open files, run dev tools, point, click, a...
desktop.summary: Users on Pro and Max plans can give Claude access to computer use. Claude can open files, run dev tools, point, click, and navigate to what’s on your screen to perform tasks itself—with no setup required. Claude’s new computer use capability makes Dispatch even more helpful by allowing it to use you

desktop.title: Control Cowork from your phone with a persistent thread (research preview for Pro/Max)
desktop.summary: Control Cowork from your phone with a persistent thread (research preview for Pro/Max)

desktop.title: Users on Pro and Max plans can access a persistent agent thread via Claude Desktop or Claude for iOS/Android to manage t...
desktop.summary: Users on Pro and Max plans can access a persistent agent thread via Claude Desktop or Claude for iOS/Android to manage tasks in Cowork. We're rolling this out to Max plans starting today, and Pro plans over the next two days. For more information, seeAssign tasks to Claude from anywhere in Cowork.

desktop.summary: Claude creates interactive charts, diagrams and visualizations

desktop.title: Claude can now create custom charts, diagrams and other visualizations in-line in its responses. For more information, s...
desktop.summary: Claude can now create custom charts, diagrams and other visualizations in-line in its responses. For more information, seeCustom visuals in chat.

desktop.title: Memory from chat history is now available for all Claude users, including free users. For more information, refer to the...

desktop.title: Use Claude’s chat search and memory to build on previous context

desktop.title: We introduced the ability to create and schedule both recurring and on-demand tasks in Cowork, as well as a new Customiz...
desktop.summary: We introduced the ability to create and schedule both recurring and on-demand tasks in Cowork, as well as a new Customize section in Claude Desktop that groups skills, plugins, and connectors in one place. For more information, seeSchedule recurring tasks in Cowork.

desktop.title: Manage Cowork plugins for your organization
desktop.summary: Manage Cowork plugins for your organization

desktop.title: Previously, Enterprise plans were only available to customers working with our Sales team. Now, any organization can pur...
desktop.summary: Previously, Enterprise plans were only available to customers working with our Sales team. Now, any organization can purchase an Enterprise plan directly on our website with no Sales conversation required. Self-serve Enterprise plans have a single seat type that includes access to Claude, Claude Cod

desktop.title: Claude Cowork — agentic mode for Desktop
desktop.summary: Claude Cowork turns Claude into a digital coworker inside the Desktop app. It can read, edit, create, and organize files on your computer — no terminal required. Available on Pro and Max plans as a research preview.

desktop.title: MCP Desktop Extensions — one-click install
desktop.summary: Claude Desktop now supports one-click installation of MCP servers, giving Claude controlled access to local tools and files. A curated Connectors Directory with admin guardrails is available for Team and Enterprise plans.

desktop.title: Claude Desktop launches on Windows
desktop.summary: Claude Desktop is now available on Windows (MSIX installer) alongside the existing macOS app. Features include inline visualizations, drag-and-drop file support, and org-level update control for staged deployment.

chrome_extension.title: Claude for Chrome now in beta for all paid plans
chrome_extension.summary: Claude in Chrome is now available to all Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise users. Features include scheduled tasks, workflow recording, multi-tab browsing, and Claude Code integration.

chrome_extension.title: Piloting Claude in Chrome — Research preview
chrome_extension.summary: Anthropic launches Claude in Chrome as a research preview with 1,000 testers. The browser extension lets Claude read, click, and navigate websites alongside you.

claude_code.title: v2.1.90
claude_code.summary: Added. Fixed an infinite loop where the rate-limit options dialog would repeatedly auto-open after hitting your usage limit, eventually crashing the session. Fixed. resume

twitter.title: New in Claude Code: Code Review — a team of agents runs a deep review on every PR
twitter.summary: New in Claude Code: Code Review. A team of agents runs a deep review on every PR. Code output per Anthropic engineer is up 200% this year and reviews were the bottleneck.

twitter.title: Can confirm Claude Code is 100% written by Claude Code
twitter.summary: Boris Cherny confirmed that Claude Code is now 100% written by Claude Code itself, up from roughly 80% in May 2025.

twitter.title: Tips for using Claude Code, sourced directly from the Claude Code team
twitter.summary: Tips for using Claude Code sourced from the team. There is no one right way to use Claude Code — everyone's setup is different.

twitter.title: How I use Claude Code — my setup might be surprisingly vanilla
twitter.summary: I run 5 Claudes in parallel in my terminal. I number my tabs 1-5, and use system notifications to know when a Claude needs input. I exclusively use Opus 4.5 — it's the best coding model I've ever used.

community.title: Ask HN: Is weird it that Anthropic raised my API limit from $500/mo to $200k?
